summary: CRITICAL FINDING: 0.60 confidence floor is UNREALISTIC and HEAVILY RESTRICTIVE. Only 34.9% of recent trades meet this threshold. Recommend 0.40-0.45 floor for realistic operation.

0.60_floor.severity: EXTREMELY HEAVY - Would cut trading frequency by >60%

0.40.assessment: Realistic - maintains reasonable trading volume while filtering worst signals

0.45.assessment: Moderate - balanced between frequency and quality

data_sources.data_used: All historical trades from trades_sports.db (SELECT only - no writes)
data_sources.recommendation_history: Not separately audited - confidence values derived from opened trades in DB
data_sources.missing_data: None detected - all trades have confidence values populated

conclusion.severity: CRITICAL - 0.60 floor is unrealistic for this system
reasoning: System has been opening trades with median confidence of 0.45
Only 35% of recent trades meet 0.60 threshold
56% of all trades fall below 0.50 confidence
Implementing 0.60 would reduce trading frequency by 65%
recommended_floor_rationale: 0.40 floor: 60% of trades pass - maintains reasonable volume with quality filter
0.45 floor: 50% of trades pass - moderate restriction with better signal quality
Both are realistic given the observed confidence distribution
conclusion.implementation_notes: If 0.60 must be deployed for risk reasons, prepare for significantly reduced trading. Consider gradually increasing floor rather than jumping to 0.60, or implement confidence-based sizing (lower confidence = smaller position) as alternative to binary gate.
